# Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector - Full Documentation

# Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Pub/Sub (Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector) enables you to connect to
the Pub/Sub API, which provides a single interface for publishing and subscribing to platform
events, including real-time event monitoring events and change data capture events. This connector
complements Anypoint Connector for Salesforce (Salesforce Connector) and provides additional ways to
interface with Salesforceâs ecosystem. The Salesforce Pub/Sub API enables you to publish and
subscribe to events and request schemas and topic information with one API.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector
Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Salesforce Pub/Sub Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Salesforce Pub/Sub target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- Anypoint Studio or Anypoint Code Builder

## Common Use Cases For the Connector

These are some common use cases for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector:

- Subscribe to event monitoring real-time events
- Subscribe to a standard or high volume platform event, such as `AppointmentSchedulingEvent`
- Publish a platform event (high volume only) back into Salesforce to restrict a userâs profile when
  they log in to Salesforce after working hours
- Subscribe to change data capture events and syn order data in an external inventory system

For examples of these use cases, refer to
[Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector Examples](salesforce-pubsub-connector-examples).

## Connection Types

Salesforce Pub/Sub connections use the following authentication types:

- Basic Authentication  
  Uses a username and a password for authentication

> [!NOTE] The `Basic Authentication` connection type is only supported for Salesforce API v64.0 and
> earlier versions.

- OAuth v2.0  
  Delegates user authentication to the service hosting the user account
- OAuth JWT  
  Uses OAuth 2.0 with a JSON Web Token (JWT) request for user authentication
- OAuth Username Password  
  Uses OAuth 2.0 with a username and a password for authentication
- OAuth SAML  
  Uses OAuth 2.0 with a signed SAML 2.0 assertion to request an access token

For information about configuring authentication types, refer to
[Configure a Global Element for the Connector](salesforce-pubsub-connector-studio#configure-global-element).

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, you are ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](salesforce-pubsub-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector Reference](salesforce-pubsub-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.3 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show several Mule flows for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector:

- [Publish an Event](#publish-an-event)
- [Configure Publish Request Timeout](#publish-request)
- [Subscribe to a Channel](#subscribe-to-a-channel)

## Configure a Connection

To secure connections, you must specify the connection field values. To do this:

- [Create a configuration file for a connection.](#create-config-file)
- [Configure the connection global elements.](#configure-conn)
- [Configure a global element for the properties file.](#configure-global)

### Create a Configuration File for a Connection

Create a configuration file that includes properties for a connection:

1.  Create a file named `mule-app.properties` in the `/src/main/resources/` folder.
2.  In the `mule-app.properties` file, create a set of properties for the connection, similar to the
    ones that follow, replacing the bracketed text (including the brackets) with the correct values
    for your configuration:

    m4-config.username=<username within Salesforce Pub/Sub system> m4-config.password=<password for
    the username within Salesforce Pub/Sub system> m4-config.securityToken=<security token within
    Salesforce Pub/Sub system> m4-config.url=<endpoint representing your development environment>

    The properties used vary depending on the selected connection configuration.

For more information about creating a properties file, refer to
[Configuring Property Placeholders](../../mule-runtime/latest/mule-app-properties-to-configure).

### Configure the Connection Global Elements

Configure global elements for connection:

1.  Create a new Mule project.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **Search in Exchange** and enter `Salesforce Pub/Sub`.
3.  Add **Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector** to the **Selected modules** section and click **Finish**.
4.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
5.  Select **Connector Configuration > Salesforce Pub/Sub Config** and click **OK**.
6.  Enter the values to configure either Basic Authentication, OAuth v2.0, OAuth JWT, OAuth Username
    Password, or OAuth SAML. Note that the `Basic Authentication` connection type is only supported
    for Salesforce API v64.0 and earlier versions.
7.  Click the **Test Connection** button to ensure there is connectivity with the Salesforce Pub/Sub
    API. A successful message should pop up.
8.  Click **OK**.
9.  Open the HTTPS Listener config in **Global Element Configuration**.
10. Click the **TLS** tab and select **TLS Configuration > Edit inline**.
11. Specify the **Key Store Configuration** with the generated keystore details to enable HTTPS on
    this configuration.
12. Click **OK**.

### Configure a Global Element for the Properties File

Configure a global element for the `mule-app.properties` file so that Mule knows where to find it:

1.  Click the **Global Elements** tab and click **Create**.
2.  In the **Choose Global Type** dialog, select **Configuration properties** and click **OK**.
3.  In the **File** field, enter `mule.app.properties`.
4.  Click **OK**.

## Publish an Event

This example Mule flow publishes an event and uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests.
- **Transform Message**  
  Outputs the data in Java.
- **Publish event**  
  Publishes the given list of events to the specified event topic. Only high-volume platform events,
  including real-time event monitoring events and change data capture events, are allowed.

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the topic used for message publishing, for example, <code>/event/gRPCMunitTestDoNotDelete__e</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Job Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>payload</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

![Studio flow for the Publish event operation](_images/publish-an-event.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:salesforce-pub-sub="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub/current/mule-salesforce-pub-sub.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties"/>

	<salesforce-pub-sub:pubsub-config name="Salesforce_PubSub_Config" >
		<salesforce-pub-sub:basic-connection username="${m4-config.username}" password="${m4-config.password}" securityToken="${m4-config.securityToken}" url="${m4-config.url}"/>
	</salesforce-pub-sub:pubsub-config>

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="3805c5f1-0f01-476a-a619-a03f34dadfdd" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
<flow name="mule-salesforce-pubsub-connector-publish-event-demo" doc:id="9e2df8f5-1b47-4a76-ac62-941c989cd93b" >
  <http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="6e06a214-3112-40ef-8720-f730121aa5e0" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/publish-event"/>
  <ee:transform>
    <ee:message>
      <ee:set-payload><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
        output application/java
        ---
        [{
          CreatedDate: 12334455,
          CreatedById: "123",
          Desc__c: "Something"
        },{
          CreatedDate: 12334456,
          CreatedById: "1234",
          Desc__c: "Something2"
        }
        ]]]>
      </ee:set-payload>
    </ee:message>
  </ee:transform>

  <salesforce-pub-sub:publish-event config-ref="Salesforce_PubSub_Config" topic="/event/gRPCMunitTestDoNotDelete__e"/>
</flow>
</mule>
```

## Configure Publish Request Timeout

This example shows how to configure the **Publish Request Timeout** and **Publish Request Time
Unit** parameters to control the timeout behavior for publish operations. This is useful when you
want to ensure that publish requests donât wait indefinitely and have specific timeout requirements.
This example Mule flow configures timeout settings.

The key timeout configuration parameters are:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Publish Request Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>60</code> (60 units of time before timeout)</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Publish Request Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code> (timeout unit specification)</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

![Global element properties window for the Publish event operation with timeout configuration](_images/salesforce-pub-publish-timeout.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:scripting="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/scripting"
	xmlns:tls="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls" xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core" xmlns:salesforce-pub-sub="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/scripting http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/scripting/current/mule-scripting.xsd http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub/current/mule-salesforce-pub-sub.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tls/current/mule-tls.xsd">
	<salesforce-pub-sub:pubsub-config name="insecureSalesforce_PubSub_Config" doc:name="Salesforce PubSub Config"  >
		<salesforce-pub-sub:oauth-user-pass-connection consumerKey="&lt;CONSUMER_KEY&gt;" consumerSecret="&lt;CONSUMER_SECRET&gt;" username="&lt;USERNAME&gt;" password="&lt;USERNAME&gt;" securityToken="&lt;SECURITY_TOKEN&gt;" tokenEndpoint="&lt;TOKEN_ENDPOINT&gt;" host="&lt;GRPC_HOST&gt;" port="0000" publishRequestTimeout="60" >
		</salesforce-pub-sub:oauth-user-pass-connection>
	</salesforce-pub-sub:pubsub-config>

	<os:object-store name="replayObjectStore" doc:name="Object store"  entryTtlUnit="DAYS" expirationIntervalUnit="DAYS" entryTtl="1"/>


	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config"  >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<flow name="ytaoka-salesforce-pubsublistenerFlow1"  initialState="started">
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener"  config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/publish"/>

	 <logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"  message="Before Publish" />
		<salesforce-pub-sub:publish-event config-ref="insecureSalesforce_PubSub_Config" topic="/event/gRPCMunitTestDoNotDelete__e">
        </salesforce-pub-sub:publish-event>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger"  message="After Publish"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## Subscribe to a Channel

This example Mule flow subscribes to a channel and uses the following operations:

- **Subscribe channel listener**  
  Subscribes to a streaming channel. This source provides channel-related notification settings for
  new events that occur after you subscribe.

  > [!NOTE] A channel must be published to Salesforce before a subscription to the channel is
  > created.

  Enter the following values:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Channel name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the streaming channel to subscribe to, for example, <code>/event/gRPCMunitTestDoNotDelete__e</code></p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Replay option</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>One of the given implementations, for example, <code>Earliest</code>. For more information on how to use <code>Replay id from object store</code> in <strong>Replay option</strong>, refer to <a href="salesforce-pubsub-connector-studio#store-objectstore">Store the Replay ID in the Object Store</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Batch events size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Total number of events included in a server batch. Lower values indicate small memory footprint with more server calls, while bigger values indicate bigger memory footprint with fewer API calls. A new batch of events is requested by the connector after the flow consumes the existing events, for example, <code>1000</code></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Subscribe channel listener** source

![Studio flow for the Subscribe channel listener source](_images/subscribe-to-a-channel.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:salesforce-pub-sub="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub/current/mule-salesforce-pub-sub.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

	<configuration-properties file="mule-app.properties"/>

	<salesforce-pub-sub:pubsub-config name="Salesforce_PubSub_Config" >
		<salesforce-pub-sub:basic-connection username="${m4-config.username}" password="${m4-config.password}" securityToken="${m4-config.securityToken}" url="${m4-config.url}"/>
	</salesforce-pub-sub:pubsub-config>

	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="3805c5f1-0f01-476a-a619-a03f34dadfdd" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<flow name="mule-salesforce-pubsub-connector-subscribe-channel-demo" doc:id="4faf2bf8-e2af-43bc-bba8-d336761800dd" >
		<salesforce-pub-sub:subscribe-channel-listener channelName="/event/gRPCMunitTestDoNotDelete__e" config-ref="Salesforce_PubSub_Config" eventBatchSize="1000">
			<salesforce-pub-sub:replay-option >
				<salesforce-pub-sub:earliest />
			</salesforce-pub-sub:replay-option>
		</salesforce-pub-sub:subscribe-channel-listener>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="21652e14-de84-4752-a9b9-871dc346befb" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)

# Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.3 - Tracking Salesforce Platform Events Example

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

One way to use Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector is to track whether a Salesforce platform event is
received from MuleSoft and processed. You can keep track of processed messages using a combination
of Salesforce and Mule runtime engine (Mule).

For a detailed example of one approach to message tracking, see
[Keeping Track of the Processed and Received Messages](_attachments/salesforce-pubsub-whitepaper.pdf).

## Replay ID and MuleSoft Object Store

To implement message tracking, itâs important to understand the core concepts of the Salesforce
Streaming API, the Replay ID attribute, and MuleSoft object store.

### Replay ID

On the Salesforce Event Bus, each published event message is assigned an opaque ID, which is
contained in the Replay (`replay_id`) attribute. The system populates the Replay ID field value when
the event message is delivered to subscribers and refers to the position of the event message within
the event stream.

The event stream has a message durability of 72 hours for high-volume platform events so, using the
Replay ID, itâs possible to receive event messages from the event stream that fall within that
period. The MuleSoft subscriber must carefully track the processed Replay IDs to ensure it doesnât
miss or duplicate process events in case of downtime or restart (either intentional or
unintentional).

To enable advanced Replay ID tracking to ensure that the event message is received and processed
correctly, you are given control over the replay behavior with the Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector in
the connector configuration UI.

This example configuration uses the **Replay id from object store** option for the **Replay option**
property and specifies a dedicated object store in the **Object Store Name** field:

![Subscribe channel listener General properties configuration panel](_images/pubsub-channel-listener-config.png)

The following table shows the Replay ID options:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Replay Option</th><th>Description</th><th>Usage</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><code>Custom replay id</code></p></td><td><p>The Subscriber receives all stored events after the event specified by its replay ID value, and new events.</p></td><td><p>Catch up on missed events after a certain event message, for example, after a connection failure. To subscribe with a specific replay ID, save the replay ID of the event message to retrieve stored events after, and then use it when you resubscribe.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>Latest</code></p></td><td><p>If no replay option is specified, this is the default. The Subscriber receives new events that are broadcast after the client subscribes.</p></td><td><p>It is best to subscribe with the <code>Latest</code> option to receive new event messages. To get earlier event messages, donât use this option.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>Earliest</code></p></td><td><p>The Subscriber receives all events, including past events that are within the 72-hour retention window.</p></td><td><p>Catch up on missed events and retrieve all stored events, for example, after a connection failure. Using this option can slow performance if a large number of event messages are stored.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><code>Replay id from object store</code></p></td><td><p>The Subscriber receives only events with a replay ID higher than the specified value in object store. If no value is found, it defaults to <code>Earliest</code>. Provide values for the <strong>Object Store Name</strong> and the <strong>Object Store Key</strong> fields to connect to the desired object store and retrieve the replay ID value.</p></td><td><p>For more information on how to use <code>Replay id from object store</code>, refer to <a href="salesforce-pubsub-connector-studio#store-objectstore">Store the Replay ID in the Object Store</a>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### MuleSoft Object Store

Mule runtime engine (Mule) uses object stores to persist data for later retrieval. Mule uses object
stores in various filters, routers, and other message processors that store states between messages.

For CloudHub deployments, the free Object Store v2 limits usage to 10 transactions per second (TPS)
per application. The premium add-on for Object Store v2 increases the limit to 100 TPS per
application.

## Example of Tracking Messages

One of the challenges of the Salesforce-provided event-based integration is knowing whether a
particular message is received and processed by the subscriber. To achieve this goal, additional
logic is required.

For details about the implementation logic for one approach to message tracking, see
[Keeping Track of the Processed and Received Messages](_attachments/salesforce-pubsub-whitepaper.pdf).

## Batch Event Size

A key feature of Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector is that it allows you to control the number of
messages received and processed in a given period of time using the **Batch event size**
(`BatcheventSize`) attribute of the **Subscribe Channel Listener** operation to specify the total
number of events included in a server batch.

### Batch Event Size Example

In a test case, 500 records were created in Salesforce using Apex code. After all the processing
completed in Salesforce, the "delta" time between creating the log entry during record creation and
the log entry from the confirmation message was analyzed and MuleSoft worker statistics were
reviewed.

In summary:

- Using `BatcheventSize=1` vs the maximum value of 100 triples the time until Salesforce records the
  processing, which is still in the area of milliseconds.
- A CloudHub 0.1 vCore worker is sufficient to handle the load. There is no performance improvement
  when using a 0.2 core worker.

For more details about this test case, see
[BatcheventSize - How Does it Work?](_attachments/salesforce-pubsub-whitepaper.pdf).

## See Also

- [Have my Published Salesforce Platform Events Been Received by My Mule App?](_attachments/salesforce-pubsub-whitepaper.pdf)
- [Event Message Durability](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/pub-sub-api/guide/event-message-durability.html)
- [Pub/Sub API and the Expanded Event Bus](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/pub-sub-api/guide/expanded-event-bus.html)
---

# Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.3 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Salesforce Pub/Sub (Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector) enables you to connect to
the Pub/Sub API, which provides a single interface for publishing and subscribing to platform
events, including real-time event monitoring events and change data capture events.

## Configuration

Default Configuration for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#PubsubConfig_Basic">Basic Authentication</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#PubsubConfig_ConfigWithOauth">OAuth v2.0</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#PubsubConfig_Jwt">OAuth JWT</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#PubsubConfig_OauthUserPass">OAuth Username Password</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#PubsubConfig_Saml">OAuth SAML</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration. Note that the <code>Basic Authentication</code> connection type is only supported for Salesforce API v64.0 and earlier versions.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Basic Authentication Connection Type

Uses a username and a password for authentication. Note that the `Basic Authentication` connection
type is only supported for Salesforce API v64.0 and earlier versions.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the client waits to receive the answer for the login request before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>60</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections. The requests to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host and gRPC port) are proxied through the proxy server configured using this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username used to initialize the session.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to authenticate the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Security Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs security token. The security token can be omitted if your IP has been added to the allowlist on Salesforce.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Web service URL responsible for user authentication. The URL for the endpoint that is configured to handle SOAP authentication requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host). The proxy requests to the gRPC host go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>api.pubsub.salesforce.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC port). The proxy requests to the gRPC port go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>7443</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time the client waits for a publish request to complete before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to 0 (no deadline).</p></td><td><p><code>0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Publish Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies how often the inactivity watchdog scheduler checks each subscriber for activity. If the scheduler detects no activity on a subscriberâs channel since the previous check, it triggers a controlled resubscription so that the subscriber can continue receiving events. Values less than or equal to 0, including blank entries, disable the inactivity watchdog.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler is a safety mechanism that detects subscribers that stop
> receiving events, for example after an unexpected gRPC stream closure, and triggers a controlled
> resubscription so event delivery can resume without restarting the Mule app. Configure the
> inactivity watchdog when subscribers in your environment occasionally stop receiving events even
> though the connection appears healthy.
>
> The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler runs only when both **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency**
> and **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit** are set. Leaving either field unset disables the
> scheduler. Set the frequency based on how often you expect to receive events. If you expect
> frequent events, a short frequency such as 5 minutes is appropriate. For lower-volume event
> streams, set a longer interval to avoid unnecessary resubscriptions.

### OAuth v2.0 Connection Type

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the client waits to receive the answer for the login request before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>60</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections. The requests to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host and gRPC port) are proxied through the proxy server configured using this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Api Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host). The proxy requests to the gRPC host go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>api.pubsub.salesforce.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC port). The proxy requests to the gRPC port go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>7443</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time the client waits for a publish request to complete before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to 0 (no deadline).</p></td><td><p><code>0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Publish Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies how often the inactivity watchdog scheduler checks each subscriber for activity. If the scheduler detects no activity on a subscriberâs channel since the previous check, it triggers a controlled resubscription so that the subscriber can continue receiving events. Values less than or equal to 0, including blank entries, disable the inactivity watchdog.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource owner ID to use with the authorization code grant type.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after receiving an access token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler is a safety mechanism that detects subscribers that stop
> receiving events, for example after an unexpected gRPC stream closure, and triggers a controlled
> resubscription so event delivery can resume without restarting the Mule app. Configure the
> inactivity watchdog when subscribers in your environment occasionally stop receiving events even
> though the connection appears healthy.
>
> The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler runs only when both **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency**
> and **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit** are set. Leaving either field unset disables the
> scheduler. Set the frequency based on how often you expect to receive events. If you expect
> frequent events, a short frequency such as 5 minutes is appropriate. For lower-volume event
> streams, set a longer interval to avoid unnecessary resubscriptions.

### OAuth JWT Connection Type

Uses OAuth 2.0 with a JSON Web Token (JWT) request for user authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the client waits to receive the answer for the login request before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>60</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections. The requests to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host and gRPC port) are proxied through the proxy server configured using this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Api Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce connected app.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Store Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password of the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Principal</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token. According to Salesforce, the <strong>Token Endpoint</strong> field is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code> or <code>https://acme.force.com/customers/services/oauth2/token</code> (in which <code>\acme.force.com/customers</code> is your community URL) if implementing for a community.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Audience Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Audience identifies the authorization server as an intended audience. The authorization server must verify that the authorization server is an intended audience for the token. Use the authorization serverâs URL for the audience value: <code>https://login.salesforce.com</code>, <code>https://test.salesforce.com</code>, or <code>https://community.force.com/customers</code> if implementing for a community.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host). The proxy requests to the gRPC host go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>api.pubsub.salesforce.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC port). The proxy requests to the gRPC port go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>7443</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time the client waits for a publish request to complete before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to 0 (no deadline).</p></td><td><p><code>0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Publish Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies how often the inactivity watchdog scheduler checks each subscriber for activity. If the scheduler detects no activity on a subscriberâs channel since the previous check, it triggers a controlled resubscription so that the subscriber can continue receiving events. Values less than or equal to 0, including blank entries, disable the inactivity watchdog.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler is a safety mechanism that detects subscribers that stop
> receiving events, for example after an unexpected gRPC stream closure, and triggers a controlled
> resubscription so event delivery can resume without restarting the Mule app. Configure the
> inactivity watchdog when subscribers in your environment occasionally stop receiving events even
> though the connection appears healthy.
>
> The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler runs only when both **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency**
> and **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit** are set. Leaving either field unset disables the
> scheduler. Set the frequency based on how often you expect to receive events. If you expect
> frequent events, a short frequency such as 5 minutes is appropriate. For lower-volume event
> streams, set a longer interval to avoid unnecessary resubscriptions.

### OAuth Username Password Connection Type

Uses OAuth 2.0 with a username and a password for authentication.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the client waits to receive the answer for the login request before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>60</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections. The requests to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host and gRPC port) are proxied through the proxy server configured using this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Api Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce connected app.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Your applicationâs client secret (consumer secret in Remote Access Detail).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username used to initialize the session.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to authenticate the user.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Security Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Userâs security token. The security token can be omitted if your IP has been added to the allowlist on Salesforce.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token. According to Salesforce, the <strong>Token Endpoint</strong> field is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code> or <code>https://acme.force.com/customers/services/oauth2/token</code> (in which <code>\acme.force.com/customers</code> is your community URL) if implementing for a community.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host). The proxy requests to the gRPC host go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>api.pubsub.salesforce.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC port). The proxy requests to the gRPC port go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>7443</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time the client waits for a publish request to complete before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to 0 (no deadline).</p></td><td><p><code>0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Publish Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies how often the inactivity watchdog scheduler checks each subscriber for activity. If the scheduler detects no activity on a subscriberâs channel since the previous check, it triggers a controlled resubscription so that the subscriber can continue receiving events. Values less than or equal to 0, including blank entries, disable the inactivity watchdog.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler is a safety mechanism that detects subscribers that stop
> receiving events, for example after an unexpected gRPC stream closure, and triggers a controlled
> resubscription so event delivery can resume without restarting the Mule app. Configure the
> inactivity watchdog when subscribers in your environment occasionally stop receiving events even
> though the connection appears healthy.
>
> The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler runs only when both **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency**
> and **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit** are set. Leaving either field unset disables the
> scheduler. Set the frequency based on how often you expect to receive events. If you expect
> frequent events, a short frequency such as 5 minutes is appropriate. For lower-volume event
> streams, set a longer interval to avoid unnecessary resubscriptions.

### OAuth SAML Connection Type

Uses OAuth 2.0 with a signed SAML 2.0 assertion to request an access token.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>30</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How long the client waits to receive the answer for the login request before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to <code>0</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>60</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Login Request Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Login Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections. The requests to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host and gRPC port) are proxied through the proxy server configured using this field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Api Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>API version used. Defaults to the latest API version supported by the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Consumer key for the Salesforce connected app.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Store Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password of the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Certificate Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the certificate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Principal</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Endpoint</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL pointing to the server responsible for providing the authentication token. According to Salesforce, the <strong>Token Endpoint</strong> field is <code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code> or <code>https://acme.force.com/customers/services/oauth2/token</code> (in which <code>\acme.force.com/customers</code> is your community URL) if implementing for a community.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Host in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC host). The proxy requests to the gRPC host go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>api.pubsub.salesforce.com</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>gRPC port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Port in which the proxy requests are sent to the gRPC API (towards the gRPC port). The proxy requests to the gRPC port go through the proxy.</p></td><td><p><code>7443</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time the client waits for a publish request to complete before timing out. Values less than one millisecond are converted to 0 (no deadline).</p></td><td><p><code>0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Publish Request Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Publish Request Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies how often the inactivity watchdog scheduler checks each subscriber for activity. If the scheduler detects no activity on a subscriberâs channel since the previous check, it triggers a controlled resubscription so that the subscriber can continue receiving events. Values less than or equal to 0, including blank entries, disable the inactivity watchdog.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler is a safety mechanism that detects subscribers that stop
> receiving events, for example after an unexpected gRPC stream closure, and triggers a controlled
> resubscription so event delivery can resume without restarting the Mule app. Configure the
> inactivity watchdog when subscribers in your environment occasionally stop receiving events even
> though the connection appears healthy.
>
> The Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler runs only when both **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Frequency**
> and **Inactivity Watchdog Scheduler Time Unit** are set. Leaving either field unset disables the
> scheduler. Set the frequency based on how often you expect to receive events. If you expect
> frequent events, a short frequency such as 5 minutes is appropriate. For lower-volume event
> streams, set a longer interval to avoid unnecessary resubscriptions.

## Sources

- [Subscribe Channel Listener](#SubscribeChannelListener)

### Subscribe Channel Listener

`<salesforce-pub-sub:subscribe-channel-listener>`

Subscribes to a streaming channel. This source provides channel-related notification settings for
new events that occur after you subscribe.

> [!NOTE]
>
> - A channel must be published to Salesforce before a subscription to the channel is created.
> - When using OAuth 2.0 authentication, the source isnât initialized during the initial application
>   startup. You must complete the OAuth authorization process and then restart the application to
>   successfully initialize the source and begin receiving events.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the streaming channel to subscribe to.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Replay Option</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>LATEST</p></li><li><p>EARLIEST</p></li><li><p>CUSTOM</p></li><li><p>REPLAY ID FROM OBJECT STORE</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Interface with the following implementations:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>Latest</code></p><div><p>Subscriber receives new events that are broadcast after the client subscribes.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Earliest</code></p><div><p>Subscriber receives all events, including past events that are within the retention window of the server and new events sent after subscription.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Custom replay id</code></p><div><p>Subscriber receives only events with a replay ID higher than the specified value.</p></div></li><li><p><code>Replay id from object store</code></p><div><p>Subscriber receives only events with a replay ID higher than the specified value in object store. If no value is found, it defaults to <code>Earliest</code>. Provide values for the <strong>Object Store Name</strong> and the <strong>Object Store Key</strong> fields to connect to the desired object store and retrieve the replay ID value. For more information on how to use <code>Replay id from object store</code>, refer to <a href="salesforce-pubsub-connector-studio#store-objectstore">Store the Replay ID in the Object Store</a>.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Batch events size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Total number of events included in a server batch. Lower values indicate small memory footprint with more server calls, while higher values indicate bigger memory footprint with less API calls. A new batch of events is requested by the connector after the flow consumes the existing events. The maximum value is <code>100</code>.</p></td><td><p><code>100</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></td><td><p><code>true</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>Non-Repeatable Stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#PubsubConfig)

## Operations

- [Publish Event](#PublishEvent)
- [Unauthorize](#Unauthorize)

### Publish Event

`<salesforce-pub-sub:publish-event>`

Publishes the given list of events to the specified event topic. Only high-volume platform events,
including real-time event monitoring events and change data capture events, are allowed.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Topic</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the topic used for message publishing. You can select the topic name from the list of available topics. Sample topic formats:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>For a standard platform event: <code>/event/EventName</code>. For a custom platform event: <code>/event/EventName__e</code>. For example, for <code>Order_Event__e</code>, the topic is <code>/event/Order_Event__e</code>.</p></li><li><p>For a change data capture channel that captures events for all selected entities: <code>/data/ChangeEvents</code>. For a single-entity channel: <code>/data/{StandardObjectName}ChangeEvent</code>.</p></li></ul></div><div><p>For more information, refer to the Salesforce documentation.</p></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Events</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>List of events to publish that match the schema of the current topic.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Maps error types thrown by this operation to custom error types.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#PubsubConfig)

#### Throws

- MULE:ANY
- SALESFORCE-PUB-SUB:CONNECTIVITY
- SALESFORCE-PUB-SUB:INVALID_INPUT
- SALESFORCE-PUB-SUB:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- SALESFORCE-PUB-SUB:SECURITY
- SALESFORCE-PUB-SUB:SERVER_ERROR

### Unauthorize

`<salesforce-pub-sub:unauthorize>`

Deletes all the access token information of a given resource owner ID so that it is impossible to
execute any operation for that user without doing the authorization dance again.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the resource owner for whom to invalidate access.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Configuration](#PubsubConfig)

## Object Types

- [CRL File](#CrlFile)
- [Custom OCSP Responder](#CustomOcspResponder)
- [Custom Replay Id](#CustomReplayId)
- [Error Mapping](#ErrorMapping)
- [Expiration Policy](#ExpirationPolicy)
- [Keystore](#KeyStore)
- [Proxy](#ProxyConfiguration)
- [Reconnect](#Reconnect)
- [Reconnect Forever](#ReconnectForever)
- [Reconnection](#Reconnection)
- [Redelivery Policy](#RedeliveryPolicy)
- [Redelivery Policy Message Identifier](#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier)
- [Repeatable File Store Stream](#RepeatableFileStoreStream)
- [Repeatable In Memory Stream](#RepeatableInMemoryStream)
- [Replay Id From Object Store](#ReplayIdFromObjectStore)
- [Standard Revocation Check](#StandardRevocationCheck)
- [TLS](#Tls)
- [Truststore](#TrustStore)

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom Replay Id

Configures a custom replay ID.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Replay Id</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Subscriber receives all events after the event specified by its <strong>Replay Id</strong> value. The value is ignored if the replay option is set on <code>Earliest</code>, <code>Latest</code>, or <code>Replay id from object store</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Error Mapping

Maps source error types to custom error types for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Source</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ANY</p></li><li><p>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TRANSFORMATION</p></li><li><p>EXPRESSION</p></li><li><p>SECURITY</p></li><li><p>CLIENT_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>SERVER_SECURITY</p></li><li><p>ROUTING</p></li><li><p>CONNECTIVITY</p></li><li><p>RETRY_EXHAUSTED</p></li><li><p>TIMEOUT</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Source error type to map.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Custom error type to map the source error to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy

Configures a proxy for outbound connections.
