<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Host in which the proxy requests are sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port in which the proxy requests are sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a <code>REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED</code> error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Identifier</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicyMessageIdentifier">Redelivery Policy Message Identifier</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine if a message was redelivered. This property can be set only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy Message Identifier

Configures how to identify a redelivered message and how to find out when the message was
redelivered.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. You can set this property only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Replay Id From Object Store

Configures a replay ID from object store.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Os Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the object store from where to get the latest replay ID. The value is ignored if the replay option is set on <code>Earliest</code>, <code>Latest</code>, or <code>Custom replay id</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Os Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the key that contains the latest replay ID. The value is ignored if the replay option is set on <code>Earliest</code>, <code>Latest</code>, or <code>Custom replay id</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Pub/Sub API Endpoints](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/pub-sub-api/guide/pub-sub-endpoints.html)
- [Use Pub/Sub API](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/platform/pub-sub-api/guide/use-pub-sub-api.html)
- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.3

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure a source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).
6.  [Configure the other connector fields](#configure-other-fields).

When you run the connector, you can view the app log to check for problems in real time, as
described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, refer to
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, refer to
the [Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector Reference](salesforce-pubsub-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with
the connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, type `Salesforce Pub/Sub` in the search field.
3.  Click <Salesforce Pub/Sub> in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure a Source

A source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these sources
to use with Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector:

- Subscribe Channel Listener  
  Subscribes to a streaming channel. This source provides channel-related notification settings for
  new events that occur after you subscribe.

  > [!NOTE] A channel must be published to Salesforce before a subscription to the channel is
  > created.

- HTTP Listener  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- Scheduler  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure a **Subscribe channel listener** source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Subscribe channel listener**.
2.  Drag **Subscribe channel listener** to the Studio canvas.
3.  Optionally change the value of the **Display Name** field.
4.  Select a **Channel Name** from the dropdown field.
5.  Select a **Replay Option** from the four types available:
    - `Latest`

      Subscriber receives new events that are broadcast after the client subscribes.

    - `Earliest`

      Subscriber receives all events, including past events that are within the retention window of
      the server and new events sent after subscription.

    - `Custom replay id`

      Subscriber receives only events with a replay ID higher than the specified value.

    - `Replay id from object store`

      Subscriber receives only events with a replay ID higher than the specified value in object
      store. If no value is found, it defaults to `Earliest`. Provide values for the **Object Store
      Name** and the **Object Store Key** fields to connect to the desired object store and retrieve
      the replay ID value. For more information on how to use `Replay id from object store`, refer
      to [Store the Replay ID in the Object Store](#store-objectstore).

6.  Optionally, specify a value for the **Batch events size** field.
7.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Connector configuration** field to configure a global
    element that can be used by all instances of the **Subscribe channel listener** source in the
    app.
8.  In the **Security** tab, optionally specify the TLS information for the connector.
9.  In the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
10. Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
11. Click **OK**.

### Store the Replay ID in the Object Store

To use the `Replay id from object store` option for the **Subscribe channel listener** source, you
must first save the replay ID value in the object store:

1.  Add the **Subscribe channel listener** source, as described in
    [Configure a Source](#configure-source), to the Studio flow.
2.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Store** from **Object Store Connector**.
3.  Drag **Store** to the Studio canvas.
4.  Optionally change the value of the **Display Name** field.
5.  Add a value for the **Key** field, such as replay ID. This is the key that is used later, when
    retrieving the replay ID from the object store.
6.  Set a value for the **Value** field. This is the replay ID value that you want to store. In this
    example, it is `payload.replayId`.
7.  Optionally, change the default values for the **Fail if present** and **Fail on null value**
    fields. The value for **Fail if present** must always be `false` as you must use the same key to
    store a new value for the replay ID.
8.  Click the plus sign (**+**) next to the **Object store** field to configure the object store.
    This is the object store in which the replay ID value is stored and from where the source
    retrieves the replay ID value.
9.  The values declared here for the **Object store** and **Key** fields are the same values that
    are used for the `Replay id from object store` option on the source.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you are specifying an action for that connector to
perform.

To add an operation for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In **Mule Palette**, select **Salesforce Pub/Sub** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas, next to the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, configure a global element that all instances of that connector in
the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the authentication credentials
that the connector requires to access the target Salesforce Pub/Sub system. Salesforce Pub/Sub
Connector supports Basic Authentication, OAuth v2.0, OAuth JWT, OAuth Username Password, and OAuth
SAML.

> [!NOTE] The `Basic Authentication` connection type is only supported for Salesforce API v64.0 and
> earlier versions.

To configure the global element for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector, follow these steps:

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the **General** configuration screen for the operation, click the **Add** icon to access the
    global element configuration fields.
3.  In the **General** tab, in **Connection**, select the authentication method to configure:
    - [Basic Authentication](#basic-authentication)
    - [OAuth v2.0](#oauth2)
    - [OAuth JWT](#oauth-jwt)
    - [OAuth Username Password](#oauth-username-password)
    - [OAuth SAML](#oauth-saml)

    You can reference a configuration file that contains ANT-style property placeholders
    (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials in the global configuration
    properties. For information about the benefits of using property placeholders and how to
    configure them, refer to
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

4.  In the **Advanced** tab, optionally specify reconnection information, including a reconnection
    strategy.
5.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
6.  Click **OK**.

### Basic Authentication

Enter the following information in the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure Basic Authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <strong>Basic Authentication</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following image shows an example of configuring Basic Authentication:

![Basic Authentication selected in the Connection section and authentication fields completed in the General tab](_images/basic-authentication.png)

### OAuth v2.0

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the global element configuration screen to
configure OAuth v2.0 authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <strong>OAuth v2.0</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Listener Config</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Callback Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authorize Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following image shows an example of configuring OAuth v2.0 authentication:

![OAuth v2.0 selected in the Connection section and authentication fields completed in the General tab](_images/oauth2.png)

### OAuth JWT

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the global element configuration screen to
configure OAuth JWT authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <strong>OAuth JWT</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the consumer key for the Salesforce connected app.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the password of the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following image shows an example of configuring OAuth JWT authentication:

![OAuth JWT selected in the Connection section and authentication fields completed in the General tab](_images/oauth-jwt.png)

### OAuth Username Password

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the global element configuration screen to
configure OAuth Username Password authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <strong>OAuth Username Password</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the consumer key for the Salesforce connected app.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Secret</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter your applicationâs client secret (consumer secret in Remote Access Detail).</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the username used to initialize the session.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the password used to authenticate the user.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following image shows an example of configuring OAuth Username Password authentication:

![OAuth Username Password is selected in the Connection section and authentication fields completed in the General tab](_images/oauth-username-password.png)

### OAuth SAML

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the global element configuration screen to
configure OAuth SAML authentication:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>User Action</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the configuration name.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Select <strong>OAuth SAML</strong>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the consumer key for the Salesforce connected app.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the path to the keystore used to sign data during authentication.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Store Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the password of the keystore.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Principal</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enter the username of the desired Salesforce user to take action on behalf of.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

The following image shows an example of configuring OAuth SAML authentication:

![OAuth SAML selected in the Connection section and authentication fields completed in the General tab](_images/oauth-saml.png)

## Configure Additional Connector Fields

After you configure a global element for Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector, configure the other required
fields for the connector. The required fields vary depending on which connector operation you use.

## View the App Log

To check for problems, you can view the app log as follows:

- If youâre running the app from Anypoint Platform, the app log output goes to the Anypoint Studio
  console window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log output goes to your
  operating system console.

Unless the log file path is customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also access the
app log in the default location `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`. You can configure the location of
the log path in the app log file `log4j2.xml`.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio)
- [Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector Reference](salesforce-pubsub-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Salesforce Pub/Sub Connector 1.3 - XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Although you can manually code a Mule app in XML, it is more efficient to use Anypoint Studio:

- If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML (either from the Anypoint Studio XML
  editor or from a text editor), you can access the connector from your app by adding reference
  statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.
- If you add the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
  connectorâs namespace and schema location and it also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `mule` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce-pub-sub/current/mule-salesforce-pub-sub.xsd
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the XML:

```xml
<mule xmlns:$2="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2"
	xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns:file="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/file/current/mule-file.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/$2/current/mule-$2.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
When you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access
to this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule4-salesforce-pubsub-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `<version>` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `Salesforce Pub/Sub`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next Step

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the
[Examples](salesforce-pubsub-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
